name: eclypse-html-report

metadata.name: eclypse-html-report
metadata.summary: A tool to generate an HTML report from the results of an ECLYPSE simulation

documentation:
![PyPI - Python Version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/eclypse-html-report)
[![pre-commit](https://img.shields.io/badge/pre--commit-enabled-brightgreen?logo=pre-commit&)](https://github.com/pre-commit/pre-commit)
[![Checked with mypy](http://www.mypy-lang.org/static/mypy_badge.svg)](http://mypy-lang.org/)
[![Checked wit pylint](https://img.shields.io/badge/pylint-10/10-green)](https://pylint.pycqa.org/en/latest/)
[![Import sorted with isort](https://img.shields.io/badge/isort-checked-brightgreen)](https://pycqa.github.io/isort/)
[![IMport cleaned with pycln](https://img.shields.io/badge/pycln-checked-brightgreen)](https://github.com/hadialqattan/pycln)
[![Code style: black](https://img.shields.io/badge/code%20style-black-black)](https://github.com/psf/black)
[![Doc style: docformatter](https://img.shields.io/badge/doc%20style-docformatter-black)](https://github.com/PyCQA/docformatter)

This release contains the `eclypse-html-report` command line tool that generates a visual HTML report from the stats produced by an [ECLYPSE](https://github.com/eclypse-org/eclypse) simulation.

## Installation

To install `eclypse-html-report` and all its dependencies, you can run the following commands:
```bash

pip install eclypse-html-report

```

**N.B.** We **strongly** suggest the installation of ECLYPSE in a virtual environment.

## Usage

```bash

eclypse-html-report --help

```

modules.name: eclypse_html_report.tools
documentation: Auxiliary functions for the HTML report generation.

functions.name: eclypse_html_report.tools.format_float
documentation:
Format a float to a string with 4 decimal places, if necessary.

```python
value = 10.123456
print(format_float(value))  # 10.1235

value = 10.0
print(format_float(value))  # 10
```

Args:

: value (float): The float to format.

Returns:

: str: The formatted string.

functions.name: eclypse_html_report.tools.to_float
documentation:
Convert a value to a float if possible.

Args:
    value: The value to convert.

Returns:
    float: The float value, or the original value if it cannot be converted.

functions.name: eclypse_html_report.tools.snake_to_title
documentation:
Convert a snake_case string to a title case string.

```python
s = "my_snake_case_sentence"
print(snake_to_title(s))  # My Snake Case Sentence
```

Args:

: s (str): The snake_case string to convert.

Returns:

: str: The title case string.

documentation: pylint: disable=missing-module-docstring

CLI tool to generate an HTML report from ECLYPSE simulation report stats.

INPUT_PATH: Required path to the report stats.

modules.name: eclypse_html_report.types
documentation: Types used in HTMLReport class.

modules.name: eclypse_html_report.node_group
documentation:
Module for the NodeGroup enumeration, which represents the group/layer of a node in
the infrastructure.

classes.name: eclypse_html_report.node_group.NodeGroup

methods.name: eclypse_html_report.node_group.NodeGroup._missing_
documentation: Return the UNSET value if the value is not in the enum.

methods.name: eclypse_html_report.node_group.NodeGroup._bool_op
documentation:
Perform a boolean operation between two NodeGroup objects.

Args:
    fn (Callable): The boolean function to be performed.
    other (NodeGroup): The other NodeGroup object to be compared with.

Returns:
    bool: The result of the boolean operation.

methods.name: eclypse_html_report.node_group.NodeGroup.__lt__
documentation:
Return True if the value of the NodeGroup is less than the value of the other
NodeGroup.

Args:
    other (NodeGroup): The NodeGroup object to be compared with.

Returns:
    bool: True if the current NodeGroup is less than the other, else False.

methods.name: eclypse_html_report.node_group.NodeGroup.__eq__
documentation:
Return True if the value of the NodeGroup is equal to the value of the.

Args:
    other (NodeGroup): The NodeGroup object to be compared with.

Returns:
    bool: True if the current NodeGroup is equal to the other, else False.

NodeGroup represents the group/layer of a node in the infrastructure. It is an enum
with the following possible values:

Attributes:
    UNSET: The node group is not set.
    IOT: The node group is an IoT device.
    EDGE: The node group is an edge device.
    CLOUD: The node group is a cloud server.

modules.name: eclypse_html_report.report
documentation:
Module for Report class, which generates the final report of the simulation.

The report can be generated in multiple formats (HTML, CSV, and JSON). Solely for HTML,
a plot type can be specified for each metric.
mypy: disable-error-code="index"

functions.name: eclypse_html_report.report._big_number
documentation:
Create a BigNumber block with the specified heading and value.

Args:
    heading (str): The heading of the BigNumber.
    value (Union[float, AnyStr]): The value of the BigNumber.

Returns:
    dp.BigNumber: The BigNumber block.

functions.name: eclypse_html_report.report._aggregated_group
documentation:
Create a Group block with two plots:
- the aggregated metric plot (application/infrastructure)
- the metric plot (service/interaction/node/link)

Args:
    fig_agg (dp.Plot): The aggregated metric plot.
    fig_by (dp.Plot): The metric plot.
    by (str): The type of the metric.
    metric (str): The metric name.

Returns:
    dp.Group: The Group block.

functions.name: eclypse_html_report.report._bar
documentation:
Create a bar plot of the DataFrame.

Args:
    df (pd.DataFrame): The DataFrame to plot.
    x (str): The x-axis column.
    y (str): The y-axis column.
    animation_frame (str, optional): The animation frame column. Defaults to DEFAULT_TICK_COLUMN.

Returns:
    dp.Plot: The bar plot.

functions.name: eclypse_html_report.report._line
documentation:
Create a line plot of the DataFrame.

Args:
    df (pd.DataFrame): The DataFrame to plot.
    x (str): The x-axis column.
    y (str): The y-axis column.

Returns:
    dp.Plot: The line plot.

functions.name: eclypse_html_report.report._scatter
documentation:
Create a scatter plot of the DataFrame.

Args:
    df (pd.DataFrame): The DataFrame to plot.
    x (str): The x-axis column.
    y (str): The y-axis column.

Returns:
    dp.Plot: The scatter plot.

functions.name: eclypse_html_report.report._select
documentation:
Create a Select block with the specified blocks. If there is only one block,
return the block.

Args:
    blocks (List[dp.Block]): The blocks to select from.

Returns:
    dp.Block: The Select block or the single block.

functions.name: eclypse_html_report.report._get_html_formatting
documentation:
Get the formatting configuration for HTML reports.

Returns:
    dp.Formatting: The formatting configuration.

classes.name: eclypse_html_report.report.HTMLReport

methods.name: eclypse_html_report.report.HTMLReport.__init__
documentation:
Create a new HTML report.

Args:
    sim_path (Union[Path, str]): The path to the simulation results.

methods.name: eclypse_html_report.report.HTMLReport.load_stats
documentation:
Load the statistics of the simulation from the configured path.

Args:
    report_range (Tuple[int, int], optional): The range of ticks to include
        in the report. Defaults to (0, MAX_FLOAT).
    report_step (int, optional): The step between report ticks. Defaults to 1.

methods.name: eclypse_html_report.report.HTMLReport.to_html
documentation:
Save the reports in HTML format.

Args:
    output_path (Path, optional): The path to save the HTML report.
        Defaults to None.
    plot_types (Dict[str, PlotType], optional): The plot types for each
        metric. Defaults to None.
    open_html (bool, optional): Whether to open the HTML report in the default
        browser. Defaults to False.

methods.name: eclypse_html_report.report.HTMLReport.simulation_page
documentation:
Create the simulation metrics page.

Returns:
    dp.Page: The simulation metrics page.

methods.name: eclypse_html_report.report.HTMLReport.applications_page
documentation:
Create the applications page with all the application metrics, for each
applicationnvolved in the simulation. Also includes the service and interaction
metrics.

Returns:
    dp.Page: The applications page.

methods.name: eclypse_html_report.report.HTMLReport.infrastructure_page
documentation:
Create the infrastructure page with all the infrastructure metrics. Also
includes the node and link metrics.

Returns:
    dp.Page: The infrastructure page.

methods.name: eclypse_html_report.report.HTMLReport.gml_page
documentation:
Create the network page with the network graph.

Returns:
    dp.Page: The network page.

methods.name: eclypse_html_report.report.HTMLReport._plot_by_type
documentation:
Plot the metric by the specified type. If no type is specified, use the
default type.

Args:
    metric (str): The metric to plot.
    default_type (PlotType, optional): The default plot type. Defaults to "bar".

Returns:
    dp.Plot: The plot of the metric.

methods.name: eclypse_html_report.report.HTMLReport._read_csv
documentation:
Read the CSV file at the specified path.

Args:
    path (str): The path to the CSV file.

Returns:
    pd.DataFrame: The DataFrame of the CSV file.

The HTMLReport class generates an HTML report for the simulation results at the
specified path.
